meaning_en: A way to wish someone good luck, especially before a performance or important event.
meaning_ko: 특히 공연이나 중요한 행사 전에 누군가에게 행운을 빌어주는 표현입니다.
example: You're going on stage in five minutes — break a leg!
tip: This idiom originated in theater culture, where saying 'good luck' was considered bad luck, so performers said 'break a leg' instead as a superstitious workaround.
